title: Get Health Insurance After 18
description: If you were in foster care, you get FREE Medicaid (Medi-Cal in CA) until you turn 26 — no income limit. Most foster youth don't know this and go uninsured.

steps.title: Know your superpower: free coverage to 26
steps.what: Former foster youth qualify for Medicaid until age 26 with no income test, thanks to the ACA.
steps.why: That's free doctor visits, mental health care, dental, and prescriptions — for years. Don't leave it on the table.
steps.action: Just confirm you were in foster care at 18 (or aged out). That's the qualifier.
steps.ai_check: Were you in foster care at 18?

steps.title: Find out if you're already enrolled
steps.what: Sometimes your Medicaid keeps going automatically after you age out — sometimes it drops. Check.
steps.why: If it's active, you might be able to use it today. If it dropped, we re-enroll.
steps.action: Call your county's Medicaid/Medi-Cal office or check your member portal. Ask me for the number.
steps.ai_check: Did you find out if your coverage is active?

steps.title: Apply (or re-apply) as a former foster youth
steps.what: There's a specific 'former foster youth' category — make sure you check that box so they don't ask for income.
steps.why: Applying under the right category skips the income paperwork and gets you approved faster.
steps.action: Apply at coveredca.com or your state Medicaid site, selecting the former-foster-youth option.
steps.ai_check: Did you submit your application under the foster-youth category?

steps.title: Pick a primary doctor and get your card
steps.what: Once approved, you choose a primary care provider (PCP) and get an insurance card.
steps.why: Having a PCP and a card means you can actually use the coverage when you need it.
steps.action: Choose a PCP in your plan and save a photo of your insurance card in your Document Vault.
steps.ai_check: Got your card saved and a doctor picked?

steps.title: Book one checkup + know mental health is covered
steps.what: Your coverage includes therapy and counseling, not just physical health.
steps.why: Using it once breaks the ice — and mental health support changes outcomes for foster youth.
steps.action: Schedule a free annual checkup, and ask about mental health services if you want them.
